name: Philippine tarsier
title: Moon-Eyed Jumper
fun_fact: Each of its eyeballs is bigger than its brain — perfect for spotting bugs at night!

name: Tamaraw
title: Mindoro Mini Buffalo
fun_fact: Only about 500 tamaraws are left in the world — all living on one island, Mindoro!

name: Visayan warty pig
title: Mohawk Forest Pig
fun_fact: This pig rocks a spiky mohawk hairstyle and uses its snout to dig for tasty roots!

name: Visayan spotted deer
title: Spotted Forest Star
fun_fact: Baby spotted deer are born covered in white dots — like a walking starry night sky!

name: Calamian deer
title: Low-Dash Deer
fun_fact: This deer is a great swimmer and loves splashing between the Calamian islands!

name: Philippine mouse-deer
title: Tiny Stripe Sprinter
fun_fact: It's one of the smallest hoofed animals on Earth — no bigger than a rabbit!

name: Palawan pangolin
title: Scaly Roller
fun_fact: When scared, it rolls into a tight ball — a living pinecone that even tigers can't open!

name: Giant golden-crowned flying fox
title: Golden Sky Giant
fun_fact: Its wings stretch wider than a 6-year-old is tall — almost 1.7 meters from tip to tip!

name: Philippine warty pig
title: Warty Snout Scout
fun_fact: Those bumps on its face aren't really warts — they're padding to protect during piggy play fights!

name: Philippine naked-backed fruit bat
title: Back-Wing Bat
fun_fact: Its wings meet in the middle of its back — making it look like it's wearing a tiny winged cape!

name: Philippine eagle
title: Forest King
fun_fact: It's our national bird! With a 2-meter wingspan, it's one of the largest and rarest eagles on Earth.

name: Philippine cockatoo
title: Red-Tail Star
fun_fact: This all-white parrot flashes bright red tail feathers when it flies — like a surprise firework!

name: Palawan peacock-pheasant
title: Sparkle Tail
fun_fact: Its tail feathers shimmer with blue-green eye spots that look just like sparkling jewels!

name: Philippine duck
title: Rainbow Wing
fun_fact: When sunlight hits its wings, they flash green, blue, and purple — a flying rainbow!

name: Philippine eagle-owl
title: Moonlight Guardian
fun_fact: Its huge orange eyes can spot a mouse from a football field away — in almost total darkness!

name: Luzon scops owl
title: Little Night Scout
fun_fact: This tiny owl is only as tall as a pencil — but its hoot can echo through the whole forest!

name: Rufous hornbill
title: Big-Bill Banner
fun_fact: Its bright red bill looks heavy but is actually hollow — like a lightweight trumpet made of keratin!

name: Visayan hornbill
title: Forest Trumpeter
fun_fact: When it flies, its wings make a loud whooshing sound you can hear from 100 meters away!

name: Mindoro bleeding-heart
title: Heart Feather
fun_fact: It has a bright red patch on its chest shaped just like a heart — nature's own Valentine!

name: Negros bleeding-heart
title: Ruby Heart
fun_fact: Its ruby-red chest feather glows in the sunlight, earning it the nickname 'the jewel of the forest.'

name: Cebu flowerpecker
title: Cebu Scarlet Spot
fun_fact: It's so rare that scientists thought it was extinct for 80 years — until someone spotted one in 1992!

name: Scarlet-collared flowerpecker
title: Mindoro Red Collar
fun_fact: Its bright red collar looks like a tiny superhero cape — and it zips through flowers just as fast!

name: Flame-breasted fruit-dove
title: Flame Chest Flyer
fun_fact: Its chest blazes with fiery orange feathers — but it's a gentle fruit eater that wouldn't hurt a fly!

name: Black-hooded coucal
title: Forest Hood
fun_fact: It wears a natural black hood like a mysterious forest detective — always watching from the bushes!

name: Cebu black shama
title: Cebu Song Star
fun_fact: Its song is so beautiful and complex that it can copy the sounds of other birds and even car alarms!

name: Philippine creeper
title: Stripe-Breast Climber
fun_fact: It climbs tree trunks spiraling upward like a tiny feathered screwdriver — never sliding down!

name: Philippine trogon
title: Ruby Belly
fun_fact: Males have a bright pink-red belly so vivid that local people call it the 'forest flame.'

name: Whiskered pitta
title: Whisker Walker
fun_fact: It has tiny feather whiskers near its beak — like a wise old forest wizard with a colorful robe!

name: Palawan tit
title: Palawan Sun Cap
fun_fact: Its bright yellow cap looks like a tiny splash of sunshine — and it never sits still for long!

name: Philippine fairy-bluebird
title: Sapphire Spark
fun_fact: Its feathers are an electric sapphire blue so intense it looks like it flew through a rainbow!

name: Philippine crocodile
title: River Guardian
fun_fact: It's one of the rarest crocodiles on Earth — with a golden-brown color that sparkles in the sun!

name: Philippine forest turtle
title: Bow-Tie Turtle
fun_fact: The pattern on its shell looks exactly like a gentleman's bow tie — very formal for a turtle!

name: Northern Sierra Madre forest monitor
title: Canopy Giant
fun_fact: It can grow longer than a baseball bat and loves climbing high into the forest treetops!

name: Panay monitor lizard
title: Panay Tree Explorer
fun_fact: This lizard is a champion climber — it spends most of its life in the trees eating fruit!

name: Gray's monitor
title: Forest Fruit Finder
fun_fact: Unlike most lizards, this one loves eating fruit — making it a forest gardener that plants seeds!

name: Philippine cobra
title: Hood Helper
fun_fact: It can spread its neck into a wide hood wider than a dinner plate — but would rather slither away than fight!

name: Samar cobra
title: Island Hood
fun_fact: Found only on Samar island, this cobra helps farmers by eating rats that damage rice fields!

name: Philippine flying lizard
title: Tree Glider
fun_fact: It can't truly fly, but it glides between trees using skin flaps — like a tiny dragon with no fire!

name: Philippine sailfin lizard
title: Sail-Back Swimmer
fun_fact: The fin on its back stands up like a sail when it swims — and it can even run across water!

name: Philippine box turtle
title: Shell Door Keeper
fun_fact: Its shell has special hinges that let it close up completely tight — like locking a tiny front door!

name: Mindoro tree frog
title: Tree-Top Hopper
fun_fact: Its sticky toe pads let it climb straight up tree trunks — no slipping, even in the rain!

name: Mindanao fanged frog
title: Forest Fang
fun_fact: Those 'fangs' are actually little bone bumps on its jaw — it looks tough but only eats bugs!

name: Philippine flat-headed frog
title: Stream Pancake
fun_fact: Its head is so flat it can squeeze under rocks in fast streams — the original pancake frog!

name: Luzon narrow-mouthed frog
title: Little Pebble
fun_fact: It's round and tiny like a pebble — perfect for hiding among rocks by the stream!

name: Philippine wrinkled ground frog
title: Wrinkle Walker
fun_fact: Its wrinkly skin helps it blend into dead leaves on the forest floor — the master of disguise!

name: Tawilis
title: Lake Silver Flash
fun_fact: It's the only freshwater sardine in the world — found only in Taal Lake, nowhere else on Earth!

name: Silver therapon
title: Silver Swimmer
fun_fact: Its shiny silver body reflects light underwater like a mirror — helping it hide from predators!

name: Sinarapan
title: Tiny Glass Fish
fun_fact: It's one of the smallest fish in the world — you could fit 10 of them on your thumb!

name: Luzon peacock swallowtail
title: Mountain Rainbow
fun_fact: Its wings flash iridescent green and blue like a peacock feather — but this beauty lives high in the mountains!

name: Golden birdwing
title: Golden Glider
fun_fact: Its gold-tipped wings are as wide as a small bird's — and they shimmer like liquid gold in flight!
